justification: Both candidates are equally invalid with zero overlap to ground-truth WSe₂ monolayer signatures. A and B hallucinate unrelated materials and peak interpretations, failing all three evaluation dimensions identically.

step1.A: Completely wrong: no WSe₂ identification, no TMD mode analysis
step1.B: Completely wrong: no WSe₂ identification, no TMD mode analysis

step2.A: Methodical for graphite but irrelevant/hallucinated for WSe₂; ignores all key diagnostics
step2.B: Methodical for ice but irrelevant/hallucinated for WSe₂; ignores all key diagnostics

step3.A: Clear but entirely incorrect output; no required WSe₂ evidence or monolayer indicators
step3.B: Clear but entirely incorrect output; no required WSe₂ evidence or monolayer indicators

thinking: Both A and B failed entirely on scientific correctness by not identifying the spectrum as monolayer WSe₂ at all. A misassigned it to bulk graphite (misinterpreting the ~249 cm⁻¹ peak as a shear mode and citing D/G bands absent in WSe₂), while B assigned it to crystalline water ice (hallucinating dominant OH-stretch bands in 3000-4100 cm⁻¹ irrelevant to TMDs). Their chain-of-thought and final outputs contain no reference to E¹₂g/A₁g degeneracy, 2LA(M) enhancement, or B¹₂g absence, making reasoning quality and completeness equally poor.
